case_design_guidance.description: Evidence-anchored guidance for aws-case-design. priority_hints is the sole channel for risk-area signals (formerly split into a separate hotspots array) — every priority_hint now carries its own confidence, same as watchlist items.

open_question_ref.description: OQ-* id whose assertion_intent decision this hint propagates. Required when assertion_intent is set.

assertion_intent.description: Propagated from open_questions_for_case_design after Step 5. Tells case-design what to assert for this pitfall — must align with the linked OQ answer. Omit on draft hints until Step 5 reconciliation; remove the entire hint when OQ decision is ignore.

suggested_scenarios.description: Case-ready scenarios derived from priority_hints. Each MUST add concrete execution detail beyond its PH (input value, asserted status/field, or precondition) — do not restate the hint. One priority_hint may fan out into multiple scenarios along distinct input/assertion dimensions.

test_strategy.description: Macro test plan proposal (scope/data/layer/approach) derived from evidence. This is a PROPOSAL for aws-case-design to confirm/adjust during its own clarifying questions — it is never asked as an explore open_question.

open_questions_for_case_design.description: Per-pitfall assertion-intent questions ONLY. Each item must trace to a discovered code pitfall (evidence_ids) and ask whether to assert the known-bug behavior, the ideal behavior, or ignore it. Macro scope/data/layer/approach questions belong in test_strategy, not here.

pitfall_ref.description: Evidence or priority-hint/watchlist id this question traces to, e.g. an SC-* source-code evidence id or PH-*/WL-* item id.

assertion_intent.description: Structured decision captured from the user's answer. null until answered.

answer.description: User's answer collected during aws-explore Step 5. null means not yet answered.

answered_via.description: Which phase collected the answer. null if not yet answered.
